npcs.name: Old Mother Wend
npcs.title: who would keep the village drowned
npcs.hail: *(a drowned matriarch sits at the head of a sunken moot-circle, water-grey and iron-spined, with the gathered authority of a soul that has held a hundred drowned generations together by sheer will — and she watches you with the wariness of an elder who has seen a great many living petitioners come to ask the dead to move aside, and has learned to distrust the word 'progress' in a stranger's mouth)*

npcs.name: Sprat
npcs.title: who would let the living drain it
npcs.hail: *(a young drowned soul — barely more than a boy when the flood took him — paces the edge of the moot-circle, restless and earnest, the only voice in a circle of the patient dead that is still thinking about the living world above the water, and still believes, with a fierceness the elders have long outgrown, that the dead owe the living something more than a drowned field nobody can farm)*

npcs.name: the Tide-Reader
npcs.title: who calls the moot, and counts it
npcs.hail: *(a soul of careful neutrality stands at the moot-circle's center with a knotted tally-cord, the one the drowned have appointed to call the question and count the voices — and it greets you not as a petitioner nor an intruder but as a possible *tie-breaker*, for the moot has been deadlocked, it will explain, for longer than the tide has a memory, and a soul from outside the circle is exactly the thing a deadlock has been waiting for)*

conversations.title: The Tide-Reader, Who Calls the Moot

variants.text: *(It runs the knotted tally-cord through careful fingers and regards your cold with a slow, considering nod.)* …A soul from *outside* the *circle.* *(it inclines its head)* *Good.* That is — unexpectedly — *exactly* what this *moot* *needs.* *(it gestures at the sunken ring of the drowned)* I am the *Tide-Reader.* I *call* the *moot*, and I *count* the *voices*, and I do *not* *take* a *side* — that is the *whole* of my *office*, and it has become a *torment*, because the *moot* has been *DEADLOCKED*, cold one, for *longer* than the *tide* keeps *count.* *(it sighs)* *Here* is the *question* before us: the *living*, *above* the *water*, want to *drain* the *flood.* This was a *village*, *once*. *Our* *village.* The *river* *broke* its *banks* a *century* past and *drowned* it — *us* — and we have *rested* *here*, *under* the *water*, *ever* *since.* *(it meets your eyes)* And *now* the *living* would *pump* the *flood* *away*, *re-claim* the *land*, *farm* the *fields* their *great-grandparents* *farmed* — which means *draining* the *water* off our *RESTING* *place*, *exposing* the *drowned* *village*, the *drowned* *DEAD.* *(it spreads its hands)* The *circle* is *split.* *Old* *Mother* *Wend* would *keep* the *flood*, our *rest* *undisturbed.* *Young* *Sprat* would *let* the *living* *have* their *land.* And I — I *count* the *voices*, and they *come* up *EVEN*, *every* *moot*, *year* after *year*, and *nobody* *wins* and *nobody* *yields* and the *living* *wait* and the *dead* *argue* and *nothing* — *nothing* — *moves.* *(it offers the tally-cord)* You're *outside* the *circle*, cold one. Which means you can *do* the *one* thing *none* of us *can*: *break* the *tie.* *Hear* them *both.* And then *tell* a *deadlocked* *moot* what a *fresh* *voice* *sees* that a *century* of *the* *same* *argument* *cannot.*

nodes.speaker: the Tide-Reader
nodes.text: *(It holds the knotted cord, patient, the keeper of a question that has not moved in a hundred years.)* Ask, cold one. *(quietly)* I am the *one* soul here with *no* *stake* but the *moot* *itself* — I want only for it to *END*, one way or the *other*, before the *living* *give* *up* *waiting* and *drain* us *without* our *leave.* *Which*, I should *say*, they are *beginning* to *threaten.* The *deadlock* is not *safe.* It only *feels* safe.

choices.text: "Lay out the real stakes, Tide-Reader. If the living drain it — what actually happens to the dead? And if they don't — what happens to the living? Both, plainly."
"A century deadlocked, evenly split. That's not an accident — a tie that holds that long is a tie somebody's *keeping.* Is the moot really about the flood, or is it about something nobody's saying?"

*(the one who can carry a voice between the living and the dead)* "Here's a thing your moot may have missed, Tide-Reader: I can cross. I carry between the living and the dead. So when Mother Wend imagines what the living want, and Sprat imagines what the dead need — they're both *guessing.* Neither side has actually *heard* the other. Before I break any tie, let me do the one thing a century of arguing couldn't: let me carry the living's true need down, and the dead's true fear up, so the moot is voting on facts instead of fears."
"Let me hear the two sides first, Tide-Reader. Wend and Sprat. Then I'll weigh in."

nodes.speaker: the Tide-Reader
nodes.text: *(It nods, and lays it out with a counter's precision.)* …*Plainly.* *(it counts on the knots)* If the *living* *drain* the *flood: the* *water* *goes*, the *drowned* *village* is *exposed* to *air* and *sun* for the *first* time in a *century* — *bones*, *foundations*, the *cairns* we *raised* *underwater* for *our* *own* *dead.* *(quietly)* For *us*, the *resting* *dead, that* *means* — *disturbance.* The *living* will *walk* where we *lie.* *Plough* it. *Build* on it. Our *rest*, which has been *deep* and *dark* and *undisturbed* for *a* *hundred* *years*, becomes a *FARM.* *(it turns a knot)* *Old* *Mother* *Wend* says *that* is a *desecration* the *dead* have *every* *right* to *refuse.* *(another knot)* And if the *living* do *NOT* drain it: the *flood* *stays*, the *drowned* *land* stays *drowned* and *useless*, and the *living* *above* — *(it meets your eyes)* — they are *not* *prospering*, cold one. The *valley's* *only* *good* *farmland* is *under* our *water.* Their *children* are *thin.* They *eke* a *living* off the *hill-slopes* while the *rich* *bottomland* their *ancestors* *worked* lies *drowned* and *forbidden.* *(quietly)* *Young* *Sprat* says *THAT* is a *cruelty* — the *dead* *hoarding* *good* *earth* the *hungry* *living* *need*, out of *nothing* but a *preference* for *undisturbed* *dark.* *(it spreads its hands)* *Both* are *right*, cold one. That is *why* the *moot* *cannot* *move.* It is not *good* *against* *evil.* It is the *dead's* *rest* *against* the *living's* *bread*, and *neither* is *worth* *less* than the *other*, and the *tide* keeps *bringing* them *up* *EVEN.*

nodes.speaker: the Tide-Reader
nodes.text: *(It goes still, and the careful neutrality flickers — you have asked the question it has wondered and never dared put to the circle.)* …*'A* *tie* somebody's *keeping.'* *(quietly)* You have a *sharp* *eye*, cold one. *(it lowers the cord)* I am *sworn* to *neutrality*, so I will say this *carefully*: a *moot* that comes up *EVEN* *year* after *year* for a *CENTURY* is not a *moot* that *can't* *decide.* It is a *moot* that does *not* *WANT* to. *(it glances at Old Mother Wend)* …*Wend* *holds* the *'keep* the *flood'* *side.* And *Wend* — *(it chooses words)* — *Wend* lost *children* in the *drowning.* *Three* of them. *Small.* They are *buried* in the *deep* of the *flooded* *village*, in the *dark* she *fights* to *preserve.* *(quietly)* I do not *think*, cold one, that *Mother* *Wend* is *truly* *arguing* about the *living's* *farm.* I think she is *arguing* about *three* *small* *graves* she *cannot* *bear* to have the *sun* *touch.* And I think *young* *Sprat* — *(it glances at him)* — *Sprat* *drowned* *young*, *angry*, *cheated* of his *whole* *life*, and I do not think *he's* truly arguing about the *living's* *bread* *either.* I think he's *arguing* that the *dead* should *stop* *clinging*, because *HE* *wants* *permission* to *stop* *clinging*, to *move* *on*, and the *flooded* *village* is the *chain* that *keeps* the *whole* *drowned* *moot* *bound* *here*, *unable* to *rest* *or* *leave.* *(it meets your eyes)* The *moot* says it's about *water.* I think it's about *grief* and *escape, wearing* *water* as a *mask.* *Find* *that* out, cold one. *Break* the *tie* by *naming* what the *tie* is *really* *made* of.

nodes.speaker: the Tide-Reader
nodes.text: *(The careful neutrality breaks into something like astonishment.)* …You can *CROSS.* *(barely)* *Carry* *between.* *(it grips the tally-cord)* Cold one — do you understand what you've — in a *CENTURY* of this *moot*, *no* one has ever — *(it gathers itself)* The *whole* *DEADLOCK*, cold one, the *thing* I could never *name* from *inside* my *neutrality* — it's *EXACTLY* what you've *said.* The *dead* *argue* about what the *living* *want*, and the *living* — *up* there, *above* the *water* — they *argue* about what the *dead* *would* *refuse*, and *NEITHER* *side* *has* *ASKED.* *(fierce)* We *guess* at each *other.* For a *HUNDRED* *YEARS* we have *voted* on *GUESSES.* *Mother* *Wend* is *certain* the *living* are *greedy* *desecrators* who'd *plough* our *graves* without a *thought.* *Young* *Sprat* is *certain* the *living* are *innocent* *hungry* *children* the *dead* are *cruelly* *starving.* And the *TRUTH* — *(it spreads its hands)* — the *truth* is *up* *there*, in the *living's* *mouths*, and *NONE* of us can *go* *ASK.* *(it presses the tally-cord toward you)* *Go.* *Carry.* Find out what the *living* *ACTUALLY* *want* — not *all* the *land*, perhaps, not the *graves*, perhaps just the *upper* *fields* — and *carry* the *dead's* *ACTUAL* *fear* — not *'no* *change* *ever'* but *'don't* *let* the *sun* *touch* the *children's* *graves'* — and *BRING* it *back* to the *moot.* *(quietly, fierce)* You will not *break* this *tie*, cold one, by *voting.* You will break it by *DISSOLVING* it — by *replacing* a *century* of *guesses* with the *one* *thing* a *moot* of the *drowned* could *never* *reach*: *what* the *other* *side* *actually* *SAYS.*

nodes.speaker: the Tide-Reader
nodes.text: *(It nods, and steps back, ceding you the circle.)* …*Hear* them *both.* *(quietly)* That is *more* than *most* *do* — the *living* *petitioners* who *come* *down* *here* *hear* only *Wend*, the *loudest* *'no'*, and *leave* *certain* the *dead* are *stubborn.* They *never* *hear* *Sprat.* *(it gestures at the circle)* *Wend* is *there*, at the *head* — the *keep-the-flood* *side*, the *rest* of the *dead.* *Sprat* *paces* the *edge* — the *let-them-drain-it* *side*, the *living's* *bread.* *(it meets your eyes)* *Hear* them *true*, cold one. And *mind* — *both* of them will *tell* you their *side* is about the *WATER.* *(a careful pause)* …*Listen* for the *thing* *under* the *water.* In a *moot* that's *held* *even* for a *century*, the *thing* that's *said* is *never* the *thing* that's *meant.* *Go.* The *circle* will *wait.* It's *very* *good* at *waiting.* It's *done* *nothing* *else* for a *hundred* *years.*

conversations.title: Old Mother Wend, Who Would Keep It Drowned

variants.text: *(She does not rise from the head of the moot-circle, only fixes you with eyes the colour of deep water.)* …Another one come to *ask* the *dead* to *move* *aside.* *(flatly)* I can *smell* it on you. Not *living* — *colder* than that — but you've the *look* of a *soul* that *brokers* *things, that* *comes* to *un-stick* what's *stuck.* *(she folds water-grey hands)* Well, *broker.* I'll *say* my *piece*, same as I've *said* it a *hundred* *years.* *(her voice is iron)* They want to *drain* us. The *living, up* *there.* *Pump* the *flood* *off* our *village* and *plant* their *turnips* where we *LIE.* And the *young* ones in this *circle* — *Sprat* and his *like* — they say *'let* them, *what* does the *dead* *need* with *land', as* if *rest* were *NOTHING*, as if a *hundred* *years* of *peace* *under* the *dark* *water* were a *thing* to be *traded* for a *stranger's* *harvest.* *(she leans forward, fierce)* I have *held* this *circle* *together* since the *flood* *took* us, cold one. *Through* every *moot*, every *young* *fool* who'd *give* our *rest* *away.* And I will *hold* it *till* the *tide* *runs* *dry.* The *dead* have *EARNED* their *dark.* *Let* the *living* *farm* the *HILLS.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mother Wend
nodes.text: *(She sits iron-spined at the head of the circle, a century of 'no' in the set of her jaw.)* Speak, then. *(flatly)* But *mind* — I've *heard* *every* *argument* the *living* and the *young* can *make.* *'The* children are *hungry.'* *'The* dead don't *need* it.'* *'It's* only *land.'* *(she shakes her head)* *None* of it *moves* me. The *dead* are *owed* their *rest.* That's the *first* law and the *last.*

choices.text: "You keep saying 'the dead's rest' — but you say it like it's bigger than the others'. What's in that flood for *you*, Wend? Not the circle. You."
"The living's children are thin, Wend. That's not an argument — it's a fact, up there, right now. How do you weigh a hundred years of your rest against a living child's hunger this winter?"

*(gently, the soul who reads what's under the water)* "Mother Wend. I'm going to say a thing the circle won't, and you can hate me for it: this was never about the farm. You lost children in the drowning. Three of them. They're down there, in the deep dark you fight so hard to keep. And you're not guarding the dead's rest. You're guarding three small graves from the sun. Aren't you."
"I'll hear Sprat's side before I weigh in, Wend."

nodes.speaker: Old Mother Wend
nodes.text: *(Her iron flickers, and for a moment something raw shows through.)* …*What's* in it for *ME.* *(she looks at the dark water)* *Careful*, cold one. That's a — that's a *closer* *question* than the *living* *ever* *ask.* *(a long pause)* …*Everything* I *had* is *down* there. *(quietly)* The *village* was my *whole* *life — born* there, *wed* there, *raised* my *babies* there. And when the *flood* *came* — *fast*, at *night*, the *banks* *just* *GAVE* — I *got* *some* of them *out.* *Not* — *(her voice catches, then hardens)* — *not* *all.* *(she straightens, iron returning)* So *yes*, cold one, the *flood* is *mine* in a way it's *not* the *young* ones'. *They* *drowned* and *lost* a *life* they'd *barely* *started.* *I* *drowned* and lost a *life* I'd *BUILT*, *whole*, *complete*, *with* *everything* and *everyone* in it. *(fierce)* The *young* can *afford* to *say* *'let* it *go, let* the *living* *have* it.'* They've *less* *down* there to *let* *go* *OF.* *(she meets your eyes)* But *don't* you *DARE* tell me my *grief* makes my *vote* *worth* *less.* The *dead* who *lost* the *MOST* should have the *LOUDEST* *voice* in what's *done* with where they *LIE.* That's — *(she falters)* — that's *only* *fair.* *Isn't* it?

nodes.speaker: Old Mother Wend
nodes.text: *(The question lands hard, and she does not dodge it, which is its own kind of honesty.)* …A *living* *child's* *hunger* *this* *winter.* *(she is quiet a long moment)* …You think I don't *FEEL* that, cold one? *(her voice roughens)* I *raised* *children.* I *fed* them off that *same* *bottomland*, *before* the *flood* — the *richest* *earth* in the *valley.* I *KNOW* what it *grows.* I *know* what *thin* *children* *look* like, *up* on the *hill-slopes*, *scraping* a *living* off *bad* *soil* while the *good* *earth* *lies* *drowned.* *(she presses water-grey hands together)* And *that*, cold one — *that* is the *thing* that *un-makes* my *sleep.* Because *Sprat's* *RIGHT*, *damn* him. The *living's* *bread* is a *REAL* *weight.* It's *not* *'only* *land.'* It's *children*, *fed* or *not* *fed*, *because* of what a *circle* of the *dead* *decides.* *(she meets your eyes, and the iron is cracked now)* So *how* do I *weigh* it? I *DON'T.* That's the *truth* I've *never* *said* in the *moot.* I *CAN'T* *weigh* a *living* *child's* *hunger* against my *dead's* *rest*, because they *DON'T* *weigh* — they're *different* *KINDS* of *thing*, and *every* *time* I *try*, the *scale* just — *swings*, and *swings*, and I *grab* the *side* that *hurts* *less* to *hold*, which is *MINE*, *my* *dead*, *my* *rest* — *(barely)* — because I *can't* *bear* the *OTHER* *side*, and *bearing* *one* *side* is *all* a *soul* can *do.* *(she looks away)* *Don't* *make* me *weigh* it, cold one. *A* *hundred* *years* I've *avoided* *weighing* it. The *avoiding* is the *only* *way* I've *stayed* *iron.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mother Wend
nodes.text: *(The iron does not crack — it shatters, all at once, a century of it, and the matriarch who held a hundred drowned generations together folds over the dark water like a struck thing.)* …*Three.* *(barely)* You *said* — you *KNOW* about the — *(she presses her hands to her face)* …*Bett.* *Corin.* *Little* *Max.* He was — Max was *two.* *(she is not iron now; she is only a mother)* They're *down* *there, cold* one. In the *DEEP.* Where the *water's* *darkest* and *coldest* and *no* *light* has *touched* them in a *HUNDRED* *YEARS.* And I — *(her voice breaks completely)* — every *moot*, every *vote*, *everyone* thinks I'm *fighting* for the *village*, the *rest*, the *DEAD's* *DUE* — and I *let* them *think* it, because the *truth* is *SMALLER* and *WORSE: I* *cannot* *let* the *SUN* *touch* my *babies' * *graves.* *(she rocks)* If they *drain* it — if the *water* *goes* — the *living* will *walk* where my *children* *LIE.* *Plough* them. *Build* on them. *Forget* them. And the *dark* — the *cold* *dark* *water* that's been *holding* my *babies* *safe* for a *century* — it'll be *GONE*, and they'll be — *exposed*, *bare*, *under* a *sky* they *never* — *(she looks at you, shattered)* …*That's* my *vote*, cold one. *That's* the *whole* of my *iron.* *Not* the *dead's* *rest.* *Three* *small* *graves* and a *mother* who *can't* *bear* the *light* to *find* them. *(barely)* …*Now* you *KNOW.* *Now* *break* your *tie.* But *gods* — *gods* — be *GENTLE* with what you *do* with it. It's the *last* *thing* I *have.*

nodes.speaker: Old Mother Wend
nodes.text: *(She nods, curtly, the iron back in place.)* …*Hear* *Sprat.* *(flatly)* Aye. *Everyone* *should*, *once.* He *means* *well*, the *boy.* That's what makes him *dangerous.* *(she looks at the young soul pacing the circle's edge)* He'd *give* it *ALL* *away — the* *village*, the *rest*, the *dark* — *gladly*, for the *living's* *thanks*, and *not* *see* what he was *trading.* *(quietly)* The *young* *always* *think* *letting* *go* is *BRAVE*, cold one. They've *not* *held* *anything* *long* *enough* to *know* what it *COSTS* to *let* it *go.* *(she meets your eyes)* But *hear* him. And then — *(a pause, and something almost vulnerable)* — and then *come* *back* to *me*, before you *count* your *vote.* *(barely)* I've *held* the *'no'* a *hundred* *years*, cold one. I'm — *(she stops herself)* …*just* *come* *back.* *Don't* *decide* the *fate* of my — of the *village* — *without* *hearing* my *side* *LAST.* The *loudest* *voice* should *also* be the *last* one, *sometimes.* It's *only* — *(she looks at the water)* — it's *only* *fair.*

conversations.title: Sprat, Who Would Let It Drain

variants.text: *(The young soul stops its restless pacing the moment it sees you, fierce and eager.)* …You're the *broker.* The *Tide-Reader* said one might *come.* *(it comes close, urgent)* *Please* — *please* *tell* me you've come to *break* the *deadlock*, because I have been *arguing* the *same* *thing* to a *circle* of *stubborn* *DEAD* for — for *longer* than I was even *ALIVE*, and *nobody* — *(it gestures at Old Mother Wend)* — *nobody* will *LISTEN.* *(fierce)* *Drain* it, cold one. *Let* the *living* have it. That's my *whole* *argument* and it's *SIMPLE* and it's *RIGHT.* *(it counts on young fingers)* The *dead* don't *EAT.* We don't *FARM.* We don't *NEED* the *bottomland — it's* the *finest* *soil* in the *valley* and we're *using* it for *NOTHING*, just *lying* in it, *drowned*, while the *living's* *children* go *THIN* on the *hills.* *(its voice cracks with earnestness)* What's *REST*, cold one, against a *hungry* *child?* What's the *dead's* *comfort* against the *living's* *BREAD?* We're *DEAD.* We had our *turn.* *(barely)* …*Most* of us *had* our *turn.* *Let* the *living* have *THEIRS.* It's the *one* *thing* the *dead* can *still* *give* the *world*, and the *old* ones *won't* *give* it, and I *don't* *UNDERSTAND* *why.*

nodes.speaker: Sprat
nodes.text: *(It paces, restless, brimming with an argument it's made a thousand times to a circle that won't move.)* *Ask*, broker. *(eagerly)* But — but *take* my *SIDE*, if you can *see* it's *right.* *Please.* I'm *so* *tired* of being the *only* *voice* in this *circle* still *thinking* about the *world* *above* the *water.*

choices.text: "You said 'most of us had our turn,' Sprat — and then you stopped. You didn't. Did you. You drowned young, cheated of your whole life. So tell me true: is this about the living's bread, or about something *you* want?"
"You call the old ones stubborn for not letting go. But letting go is easy when you've less to lose. Have you asked Wend what she's holding onto? Or just decided she's wrong?"

*(gently, the soul who reads what's under the water)* "Sprat. I think your argument's a mask — a true thing you hide a truer thing behind. You don't really care about the living's turnips. You're using their hunger because it's the one argument that might *unbind* the moot — and you want it unbound because the flooded village is the chain that keeps the whole drowned circle here, unable to rest or leave. You don't want to free the land, Sprat. You want permission to move on. Don't you."
"I've heard you, Sprat. Let me weigh it against the rest."

nodes.speaker: Sprat
nodes.text: *(It stops pacing, and the fierce earnestness wavers into something more naked.)* …*'Is* it about *me.'* *(barely)* …*Nobody's* — *nobody* in the *circle* has *ever* *asked* me *that.* They just hear *'drain* it, *feed* the *living'* and they think I'm — *noble*, or *foolish*, or *young.* *(it looks at the dark water)* …I was *fourteen*, cold one. When the *flood* *took* me. *Fourteen.* I'd never — I'd never *done* *anything.* Never *worked* a *field* of my *own*, never *kissed* anyone, never — *(its voice cracks)* — never *LIVED.* I just *drowned*, in the *dark*, *fourteen*, with my *whole* *life* a — a *blank* *page* the *flood* *took* before I could *write* on it. *(it meets your eyes, fierce and wounded)* And *yes.* *YES*, cold one. There's a — there's a *thing* in me that *can't* *STAND* it. The *resting.* The *old* ones, *content* to *lie* in the *dark* *forever*, *satisfied* — because they *LIVED*, they *had* their *whole* *lives*, they can *AFFORD* to *rest.* *(barely)* But I *didn't.* And *lying* *still* in the *dark* *water* for a *CENTURY*, *resting* a *life* I never got to *LIVE* — it's not *rest* for *me*, cold one. It's a *SECOND* *drowning.* It's the *flood* *taking* me *AGAIN*, *slower.* *(it looks at the water, fierce)* So when I say *'drain* it' — *maybe* it's not *all* about the *living's* *bread.* *Maybe* it's that I *can't* *bear* to *lie* in that *water* *one* *more* *century*, *resting* a *life* I never *had* — and *draining* it is the *only* *way* I can *imagine* *anything* *CHANGING.* *(barely)* …*Is* that *selfish*, cold one? I *thought* I was *fighting* for the *living's* *children.* Maybe I'm just — *fighting* to *not* *drown* *forever.*

nodes.speaker: Sprat
nodes.text: *(It bristles, then falters — the question catches it off-guard.)* …*Asked* her. *(slowly)* What she's — *holding* onto. *(it glances at Old Mother Wend, and the certainty wavers)* …*No.* *(quietly)* I *haven't.* I just — I *decided* she was *stubborn.* *Old.* *Clinging.* *Afraid* of *change* the way *old* *things* *are.* *(it looks at the dark water)* …I *never* *asked* what was *DOWN* there, for *her.* What she's — *(it stops)* …*Everyone* in this *circle* *lost* *something* in the *drowning*, didn't they. I've been so — so *LOUD* about *MY* *loss, the* *life* I *never* *had — that* I never — *(it winces)* …*gods.* I've been *arguing* with *Mother* *Wend* for a *CENTURY* and I don't *KNOW* what she *lost.* I just *know* it makes her *vote* *'no', and* I *resent* the *'no'*, so I never *asked* what was *behind* it. *(it meets your eyes, abashed)* …That's — that's *not* *fair*, is it. To *argue* a *century* with a *soul* and never *ask* what she's *fighting* *FOR.* *(quietly)* *Maybe* — maybe the *deadlock* isn't *her* *stubbornness, cold* one. *Maybe* it's that *neither* of us has *EVER* *asked* the *other* what's *really* *down* in that *water.* We just — *vote* our *own* *grief* and *resent* that the *other* *side* *won't* *yield.* *(barely)* …*Ask* her, cold one. What she's *holding.* I'm — I'm *afraid* if *I* *ask*, after a *century* of *fighting* her, she'll think it's a *trick.* But *you* could. And *maybe* — *maybe* if I *KNEW* — *(it falters)* …I don't *know.* But I'd *rather* *know* than *win.* That's — that's *new.* I've *only* ever *wanted* to *win.*

nodes.speaker: Sprat
nodes.text: *(It goes very still — caught, seen all the way through — and then the fierce restlessness collapses into something young and raw and relieved.)* …*'Permission* to *move* *on.'* *(barely)* …*Yes.* *(it sinks down)* *Gods.* *Yes.* You *SEE* it. *(it presses young hands to its face)* I *don't* care about the *turnips, cold* one. I'm *SORRY*, but I *don't* — the *living's* *bread* is — it's a *GOOD* *argument*, it's *TRUE*, the *children* *are* *thin* — but it's not *WHY.* *(it looks at the dark water, the drowned village)* The *WHY* is — this *flood* is a *CHAIN, cold* one. *All* of us, *bound* to the *drowned* *village*, *unable* to *rest* *PROPER* because the *village* *won't* *settle*, and *unable* to *LEAVE* because — because *Mother* *Wend* and the *old* ones *HOLD* us, *keep* the *circle* *together*, *keep* us *all* *here* in the *dark* *water*, *waiting*, *waiting*, for a *hundred* *years.* *(fierce, breaking)* And I *want* *OUT.* I want to *move* *on*, *through* the *good* *door*, to *whatever's* *next* — to *finally* *LIVE* the *thing* I never — and I *CAN'T*, because the *moot* *binds* us, because *Wend* *won't* *let* the *circle* *break.* *(it meets your eyes)* So I *argue* *'drain* the *flood'* — because if the *village* is *DRAINED*, *exposed*, *FINISHED*, then there's *nothing* *left* to *bind* us *to*, and the *circle* *breaks*, and we're all — *free.* To *rest* or to *go.* *(barely)* I've been *using* the *living's* *hunger* to *argue* for my *own* *escape*, cold one. *That's* the *ugly* *truth* of it. And I *didn't* even — I didn't even *KNOW* it, till you *said* it. *(it looks at Wend, new)* …*She's* doing the *same* *thing*, isn't she. *Arguing* *water* when she *means* *grief.* We *both* are. *A* *whole* *moot* of souls *voting* their *secret* *hearts* and *calling* it a *vote* about a *FIELD.*

nodes.speaker: Sprat
nodes.text: *(It nods, restless, and resumes its pacing, but watches you with eager hope.)* …*Weigh* it. *(fervent)* Aye. *Weigh* it *true.* *(it gestures up, toward the living world above the water)* And *mind* — when you *weigh* — *remember* there's a *whole* *world* *up* *there* that *DOESN'T* *get* a *voice* in this *circle.* The *living.* The *hungry* *ones.* *They* can't *come* *down* and *argue* the *moot* — they just *WAIT*, up *there*, on the *bad* *soil*, *hoping* the *dead* will *yield.* *(it meets your eyes)* *Somebody* has to *speak* for *them*, cold one, *down* here in the *dark* where *only* the *DEAD* *vote.* I've *been* *trying.* *Badly*, maybe — *(a flicker of the new self-knowledge)* — *maybe* for *wrong* *reasons.* But *trying.* *(quietly)* If you *weigh* this *moot* and *forget* the *living* *get* *no* *vote* in it — then the *deadlock* *wins, and* the *children* *stay* *thin*, and the *dead* *argue* *forever* in the *dark.* *Don't* let the *deadlock* *win, cold* one. *Even* if my *reasons* are — *muddier* than I *thought.* The *living's* *hunger* is *still* *real.* *Somebody's* *got* to *count* it.
